# unifi_firewall_policy (Resource)

A zone-based firewall policy on the UniFi controller. A policy governs traffic from a source firewall zone to a destination firewall zone.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Block the IoT zone from initiating traffic into the Trusted zone.
resource "unifi_network" "iot" {
  name    = "IoT Devices"
  vlan_id = 20
  enabled = true
}

resource "unifi_network" "trusted" {
  name    = "Trusted LAN"
  vlan_id = 50
  enabled = true
}

resource "unifi_firewall_zone" "iot" {
  name        = "IoT"
  network_ids = [unifi_network.iot.id]
}

resource "unifi_firewall_zone" "trusted" {
  name        = "Trusted"
  network_ids = [unifi_network.trusted.id]
}

resource "unifi_firewall_policy" "block_iot_to_trusted" {
  name            = "Block IoT to Trusted"
  enabled         = true
  logging_enabled = true
  description     = "IoT devices may not initiate connections into the trusted LAN."

  action = {
    type = "BLOCK"
  }

  ip_protocol_scope = {
    ip_version = "IPV4_AND_IPV6"
  }

  source = {
    zone_id = unifi_firewall_zone.iot.id
  }

  destination = {
    zone_id     = unifi_firewall_zone.trusted.id
    network_ids = [unifi_network.trusted.id]
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `action` (Attributes) Action applied to matched traffic. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action))
- `destination` (Attributes) Traffic destination. Set at most one of network_ids, ip_addresses, ports, or domains. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--destination))
- `ip_protocol_scope` (Attributes) IP version and protocol matching scope. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ip_protocol_scope))
- `name` (String) Firewall policy name.
- `source` (Attributes) Traffic source. Set at most one of network_ids, ip_addresses, or ports. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source))

### Optional

- `connection_state_filter` (List of String) Match on firewall connection state. Any of NEW, INVALID, ESTABLISHED, RELATED. Omit to match all states.
- `description` (String) Free-form policy description.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the policy is enabled.
- `ipsec_filter` (String) Match IPsec-encrypted traffic: MATCH_ENCRYPTED or MATCH_NOT_ENCRYPTED. Omit to match all traffic.
- `logging_enabled` (Boolean) Generate syslog entries when traffic matches this policy.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Firewall policy UUID.
- `index` (Number) Evaluation order index assigned by the controller.
- `origin` (String) Policy origin as reported by the controller (e.g. USER_DEFINED, SYSTEM_DEFINED).

<a id="nestedatt--action"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action`

Required:

- `type` (String) Action: ALLOW, BLOCK, or REJECT.

Optional:

- `allow_return_traffic` (Boolean) For ALLOW actions, automatically allow return traffic on the mirrored zone pair. Ignored for BLOCK/REJECT.


<a id="nestedatt--destination"></a>
### Nested Schema for `destination`

Required:

- `zone_id` (String) UUID of the firewall zone the traffic is destined to.

Optional:

- `domains` (List of String) Domains to match as the destination.
- `ip_addresses` (List of String) IP addresses to match as the destination.
- `network_ids` (List of String) UUIDs of networks to match as the destination.
- `ports` (List of Number) Destination ports to match.


<a id="nestedatt--ip_protocol_scope"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ip_protocol_scope`

Required:

- `ip_version` (String) IP version to match: IPV4, IPV6, or IPV4_AND_IPV6.

Optional:

- `protocol` (String) Named protocol to match (e.g. tcp, udp, tcp_udp, icmp, icmpv6). Omit to match all protocols.


<a id="nestedatt--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source`

Required:

- `zone_id` (String) UUID of the firewall zone the traffic originates from.

Optional:

- `ip_addresses` (List of String) IP addresses to match as the source.
- `network_ids` (List of String) UUIDs of networks to match as the source.
- `ports` (List of Number) Source ports to match.
